best_window: Early morning to late morning before wind builds
report_text:
The model expects 10-14 ft of visibility, resulting in a C grade. Conditions are moderately favorable overall, but the algorithm is seeing enough swell energy, surface movement and wind-driven mixing to prevent a clearer B-grade forecast.

The falling tide is an additional negative signal. As water moves toward the next low tide at 10:03 PM, visibility may gradually decline because the outgoing tide is less likely to bring cleaner offshore water into La Jolla.

Overall, the forecast remains diveable, but clarity may vary by location and could be worse around shallow reefs, sandy bottoms and areas exposed to surge.

best_window: Early morning to late morning before wind builds
report_text: Viz is currently sitting around 10-14 ft. Primary swell is around 1.8 ft @ 6s from W, so expect a little texture and surge around exposed reef. Wind tops out near 11 mph, enough to add some afternoon bump but not totally wreck the day. Workable, but expect some haze and pick your entry carefully. Stay safe out there divers.
